name: show_help
description: Displays the list of slash commands. Use ONLY when the user explicitly requests a command list or help menu. Do NOT use for general questions (about analysis methods, roles, opinions, etc.) — respond with text directly in those cases.

name: generate_report
description: Generate a formatted report file (markdown/json/html) from findings you have ALREADY gathered this turn — pass them in analysis_data. Use when the user asks for a report, write-up, or document deliverable. For raw machine-readable output use export_json; for a quick answer the user will just read in chat, write prose inline instead of creating a file.

analysis_data.description: Structured analysis results to include in the report

format.description: Output format for the report. Defaults to markdown.

name: export_json
description: Export structured data to a JSON file artifact on disk. Use when the user asks to save results as JSON, export machine-readable output, or persist structured data. For a human-readable document use generate_report; for arbitrary text/code files use write_file.

filename.description: Output filename. Defaults to a timestamped GEODE export name.

output_dir.description: Output directory path. Defaults to the current directory.

name: check_status
description: Report GEODE runtime status: connected/inactive MCP servers, available tools, current model, and config. Use when the user asks about system or health status, the MCP list, which tools are available, or 'what's connected'. Do NOT call it just to name the current model — that is already in your model card; answer directly.

name: switch_model
description: Switch the active LLM model or ensemble mode (e.g. 'use opus', 'switch to gpt', 'cross-check'). Omit model_hint to show the picker. This only swaps the active model — for login, subscription plans, API keys, or provider routing use manage_login instead.

model_hint.description: Desired model or mode hint (e.g. 'opus', 'haiku', 'gpt', 'cross', 'ensemble'). If omitted, displays the model selection menu.

name: session_search
description: Searches past conversation messages via SQLite FTS5 (Hermes Phase 1d + 1d.2). scope='project' (default) searches the current project's sessions.db (trigram-aware via prefer_trigram). scope='all' searches the cross-project ~/.geode/search/global.db rebuilt by 'geode reindex' — results include project_id/project_slug so the agent can tell where each hit came from. Returns matching messages with snippets and bm25 relevance scores. Use this to recall prior conversation context the LLM cannot remember from earlier turns.

query.description: Search query. Hyphens / dots / special chars are auto-sanitised; pass natural text or identifiers.

scope.description: 'project' = current project's sessions.db (default). 'all' = cross-project ~/.geode/search/global.db rebuilt by 'geode reindex'.

session_id.description: Optional — restrict search to a single session by id. Honoured by both scopes.

project_id.description: Optional — restrict scope='all' results to one project. Ignored when scope='project' (the active session manager already pins the project).

prefer_trigram.description: scope='project' only — query the trigram index (substring + CJK partial-word recall). Falls back to unicode61 when trigram is unavailable. scope='all' always uses unicode61.

include_artifacts.description: Also search synthesized long-context artifacts such as compaction summaries and dreams. Default false.

artifact_kinds.description: Optional artifact kind filter when include_artifacts=true, for example ['compaction_summary', 'dream'].

name: memory_search
description: Search GEODE's curated memory — saved insights, rules, and prior results — across tiers (session/project/organization). Use when the user asks what GEODE has learned or recorded before. Siblings: note_read for the user's own saved notes, session_search for the raw transcript of past conversations. This searches distilled memory, not chat logs.

tier.description: Memory tier to search. Default: all

name: memory_save
description: Save a GEODE-derived insight or fact to memory under a key, to recall in later sessions (e.g. key 'project_insight'). Read it back with memory_search. Sibling: note_save stores the user's own dictated content verbatim ('remember my flight is at 9') — use memory_save for knowledge GEODE distilled, note_save for the user's jottings.

key.description: Identifier key for the data to save (e.g. 'project_insight')

name: manage_rule
description: Create, update, delete, or list runtime rules — named, path-scoped guidance GEODE applies while working. Use when the user wants to add, edit, remove, or see active rules. action is required; create/update also need name and content.

paths.description: Subject or file patterns the rule applies to.

name: set_api_key
description: Sets a single PAYG API key. Prefer manage_login for richer flows (subscription plans, OAuth, routing). Use this only when the user pastes a raw key without context.

key_value.description: API key value (if omitted, displays current status)

name: manage_auth
description: Legacy auth-profile manager. Prefer manage_login. Retained for the existing /auth interactive add path.

sub_action.description: Action to perform (add, remove, list). If omitted, displays status.

name: manage_login
description: Unified credentials/plans command — Plans + API keys + OAuth + routing in one tool. Use whenever the user says login/logout, sets up a subscription (GLM Coding Lite/Pro/Max, ChatGPT Plus/Pro/Pro Lite/Team/Business/Enterprise/Edu, Claude Pro/Max ×5/Max ×20/Team), changes credentials, asks about quota, switches active plan, or pastes an API key with intent. Mirrors the /login slash command (see `core.cli.commands.cmd_login`).

subcommand.description: One of: status (default — show plans/profiles/routing/quota), openai (ChatGPT subscription OAuth), anthropic (Claude subscription OAuth), add (interactive wizard), set-key, use, route, remove, quota, help.

args.description: Optional positional arguments for the subcommand. Examples: openai → ''; anthropic → ''; set-key → 'glm-coding-lite zai-xxxx'; use → 'glm-coding-lite'; route → 'glm-5.1 glm-coding-lite glm-payg'; remove → 'glm-coding-lite'.

name: calculate
description: Compute bounded arithmetic exactly-first in GEODE's local runtime. Use for deterministic numeric calculation instead of run_bash or ad hoc Python. Supports +, -, *, /, //, %, **, parentheses, abs(), and sqrt(); returns exact fractions when possible and decimal output at requested precision.

precision.description: Decimal digits for approximate output. Default 50.

name: generate_data
description: Generate synthetic sample data for testing or demos. Use ONLY when the user explicitly asks for test, sample, mock, or dummy data. Never use it to answer a real question or fetch real data — use general_web_search or a real source for that.

count.description: Number of subjects to generate (default: 5, max: 20)

genre.description: Optional category/genre filter for the generated samples.

name: schedule_job
description: Manage scheduled jobs that run a natural-language action on a timed or recurring basis. For sub_action=create you MUST supply both expression (WHEN, e.g. 'daily at 9:00', 'every 5 minutes') and action (WHAT — a prompt the AgenticLoop runs at fire time, e.g. 'search new AI papers and post a digest'). Use for 'remind me', 'every morning', 'schedule X'. For a one-off thing to do now, just do it; for named event/cron triggers use trigger_event; for putting an entry on the user's calendar use calendar_create_event.

expression.description: Schedule expression in English. Examples: 'every 5 minutes', 'daily at 9:00', 'every monday at 9:00'. Required for create.

action.description: What to do when fired — a natural language prompt. Example: 'Search AI papers and create digest'. REQUIRED for create.

name: trigger_event
description: Manage event/cron triggers or fire one manually. Use when the user wants to list triggers, fire a named event, or run a trigger now. Omit sub_action to list. Sibling: schedule_job creates time-based recurring jobs (this fires named events).

sub_action.description: Action to perform (list, fire). If omitted, displays list.

event_name.description: Name of the event to fire (for fire action)

name: run_bash
description: Execute a shell command on the user's local machine. REQUIRES user approval before execution. Use for: system checks, package installs, process management, git operations. PREFER dedicated tools over bash: file search → glob_files, content search → grep_files, read files → read_document, edit files → edit_file, open URLs → playwright MCP (browser_navigate). DO NOT use for: destructive operations, privilege escalation, or finding/launching browsers (use playwright MCP instead).

name: computer_use
description: Emulated desktop control through a normal function-call tool, used when the active model/backend cannot use a provider-native computer tool. Requires user approval and computer-use opt-in. Workflow: call action='capture' to record a compact screen observation; when visual targeting is needed, call action='locate' with a concise instruction only if visual grounding is supported for the active provider/source. Locate uses only the active provider/source's configured grounding path and will not silently fall across providers (for example, OpenAI subscription will not spend GLM credits). If locate is unsupported, use source-safe helpers such as ui_probe, browser DOM tools, playwriter for login-required Chrome sessions, or keyboard navigation; do not blind-type until focus/target has been independently verified. Then use click/type/key/scroll/drag and verify with capture_after. This tool intentionally omits raw screenshot base64 from results to keep normal function-call contexts small and wire-safe.

action.description: Action to perform. Use capture first. Use locate when the model needs coordinates for a described visual target.

instruction.description: For action='locate': concise visual target description, e.g. 'the blue Submit button'.

x.description: Target-space x coordinate, usually returned by locate. Required for click/move/scroll.

y.description: Target-space y coordinate, usually returned by locate. Required for click/move/scroll.

button.description: Mouse button for click. Defaults to left.

click_count.description: Click count for action='click'. Defaults to 1.

text.description: Text to type for action='type'. Dangerous shell patterns are blocked.

keys.description: Key or key combo for action='key', e.g. 'cmd+s', 'ctrl+c', 'return'. Destructive system shortcuts are blocked.

amount.description: Scroll amount in wheel ticks. Defaults to 3.

capture_after.description: Whether action results should include compact post-action observation metadata. Defaults to true.

name: delegate_task
description: Delegates a task to a sub-agent for parallel execution. Use when: (1) analyzing multiple subjects simultaneously, (2) running independent subtasks in parallel, (3) long-running tasks that shouldn't block the main conversation. Each sub-agent gets its own context and tool access. For sequential single tasks, execute directly instead.

task_description.description: Description of the task to execute (single task mode)

role.description: Optional built-in capability role. Restricts the sub-agent to the role's tool allowlist and validates its result against the role's output schema (repo_researcher: read-only research findings; patcher: file edits + patch list; verifier: run_bash checks + pass/fail; reviewer: read-only review findings). Omit for the default unrestricted sub-agent.

best_of.description: Single-task mode only: run N candidates of the SAME task in parallel, each with a distinct built-in reasoning lens, then return the judge-selected winner in a 'best_of' result block alongside all candidates. Costs N sub-agent runs plus one judge LLM call — use when the task is verification-friendly (clear correctness criteria) and worth the extra compute. Ignored in batch mode.

tasks.description: Batch mode: array of tasks to run in parallel

name: update_plan
description: Show or update the current turn's progress checklist. Use for complex, multi-step work to keep the user informed while continuing execution. This is NOT an approval gate and does not persist an execution plan; after calling it, keep working unless the user explicitly asked to stop for review. Keep 3-7 concise steps, exactly one in_progress step while work remains, and update statuses as steps complete. Sibling: create_plan is only for an explicit review checkpoint that should wait for approve_plan.

explanation.description: Optional short reason for a plan change.

plan.description: Complete replacement checklist for this turn.

name: create_plan
description: Create a disk-persistent review checkpoint that waits for approve_plan before execution. Use only when the user explicitly asks to review/approve a plan first, or when you need an intentional stop before an unusually risky/expensive workflow. For ordinary multi-step work, use update_plan and continue executing; do not turn routine planning into a permission prompt.

goal.description: Execution goal (e.g. 'Research AI trends and write a report')

steps.description: List of plan steps (e.g. ['web search', 'data collection', 'analysis', 'report writing']). Auto-generated if omitted.

subject.description: Optional subject label for the plan

name: approve_plan
description: Approve and run a review checkpoint created by create_plan. Use only after the user explicitly accepts that checkpoint; pass the plan_id from the create_plan result. Siblings: modify_plan to adjust it first, reject_plan to discard it. Do not use for update_plan checklists.

plan_id.description: ID of the plan to approve (from create_plan result)

name: llms_txt_index
description: Fetches a documentation site's /llms.txt index (llmstxt.org convention) and returns it parsed into structured sections of links: title, summary, sections[].links[{name, url, notes, same_origin}]. Use this FIRST when researching a documentation site, then web_fetch only the relevant linked pages. Accepts a site or base URL (probes <path>/llms.txt then <origin>/llms.txt) or a direct llms.txt URL. Unlike web_fetch, the full index is parsed without the 10k-char truncation. If the site publishes no llms.txt, returns not_found with a fallback hint (general_web_search scoped to the site).

url.description: Documentation site URL (base URL or direct llms.txt URL)

max_links.description: Maximum links to return across sections (default: 200, max: 1000); excess sets truncated=true

name: web_fetch
description: Fetches text content from a URL (returns plain text, NOT a visual browser). Use when the user provides a URL and wants to read its content. To visually open a URL in a browser, use playwright MCP (browser_navigate) instead. To search the web, use general_web_search instead. For documentation sites, use llms_txt_index first - it fetches and parses the site's /llms.txt index (llmstxt.org convention); then web_fetch only the relevant linked pages. llms-full.txt, when present, holds the entire docs in one file (large).

max_chars.description: Maximum characters to return (default: 8000, max: 10000). Do not exceed 10000.

name: browser_scan
description: Perceive a REAL Chrome tab over the DevTools Protocol: lists open tabs and returns a compacted text view of a page (repeated lists collapsed). Unlike web_fetch (headless GET, no JS), this reads the operator's own logged-in browser — cookies, sessions, and rendered SPA content are all present. Requires Chrome launched with --remote-debugging-port=9222. Use browser_execute_js to act on the page.

url_contains.description: Pick the tab whose URL contains this substring; omit to use the active tab.

tabs_only.description: Return only the tab list, no page content (saves tokens). Default: false.

max_chars.description: Max characters of page content to return (default 8000, cap 35000).

name: browser_execute_js
description: Execute JavaScript in a REAL Chrome tab over the DevTools Protocol and return its value. Full control of the operator's logged-in browser (click, fill, navigate via location.href, read DOM). Top-level await is supported; return a serialisable value. Requires Chrome launched with --remote-debugging-port=9222.

script.description: JavaScript expression to evaluate in the tab. Its value is returned.

url_contains.description: Pick the tab whose URL contains this substring; omit to use the active tab.

name: ui_probe
description: Perceive a macOS app's UI structurally via the accessibility (AX) tree — each control's role, title, value, enabled state, and on-screen rectangle as compact text. A cheaper, more reliable first step than a computer_use screenshot for 'what is on screen' on native macOS apps. Falls back to computer_use screenshots when AX is unavailable (games, custom-drawn canvases). Requires macOS Accessibility permission. Rectangles are AX logical points (coord_space=ax_points); mapping to a click coordinate is not yet calibrated.

app_name.description: App to inspect (localized name or bundle id). Omit to use the frontmost app.

name: general_web_search
description: Searches the web for up-to-date information. Dispatches through the adapter registry's WebSearchCapable chain (provider preference Anthropic -> OpenAI -> GLM, with subscription endpoints promoted ahead of PAYG per the operator's credential_source setting). Prefer this tool when the user asks about current information, news, or trends. This is a text search — for browsing a specific site visually, use playwright MCP (browser_navigate) instead.

name: read_document
description: Reads a local file (markdown, text, JSON). Use when the user wants to view local file contents. For remote URLs, use web_fetch instead. For file discovery, use glob_files first.

file_path.description: Path to the file to read. Symlinks allowed if they resolve within the project directory.

offset.description: Line number to start reading from, 1-indexed (default: 1)

limit.description: Number of lines to read. Omit for full file (subject to 256KB size limit).

max_lines.description: Deprecated — use 'limit' instead. Maximum lines to read.

name: document_ingest
description: Ingest a local PDF into a GEODE-readable bundle under .geode/documents/<doc_id>. Use this when the user asks GEODE to read, analyze, parse, summarize, OCR, or use a PDF as context. Backends are provider-specific: local_text extracts embedded text with pdftotext, openai_pdf uses OpenAI Responses input_file, claude_pdf uses Claude PDF document blocks, and zhipu_ocr uses Z.ai GLM-OCR layout_parsing. Prefer local_text for text-layer PDFs; use provider backends for visual layout, scans, charts, tables, or OCR.

file_path.description: Path to a local PDF inside the GEODE file sandbox.

backend.description: Backend to use. auto tries local text first, then the current provider family if local extraction is empty.

task_prompt.description: Provider prompt for PDF parsing or extraction.

model.description: Provider model override for OpenAI or Claude backends.

output_dir.description: Optional output directory. Defaults to .geode/documents/<doc_id> under the project root.

page_range.description: Optional 1-indexed PDF page range, e.g. '1-100' or '1-25,40,80-120'. GEODE accepts wide ranges and applies provider-specific chunking where needed.

page_chunk_size.description: Optional provider chunk size in pages. Zhipu defaults to 100 pages per request, with a 30-page retry fallback.

start_page.description: Deprecated compatibility alias for page_range. First PDF page, 1-indexed.

end_page.description: Deprecated compatibility alias for page_range. Last PDF page, 1-indexed.

name: glob_files
description: Find files by glob pattern (e.g. '**/*.py', 'core/**/*.json'). Results sorted by modification time. ALWAYS prefer this over run_bash for file discovery (ls, find). To search file contents, use grep_files instead.

path.description: Directory to search in (default: project root) Must be within the project directory — absolute paths outside the project are blocked.

name: grep_files
description: Search file contents using regex patterns. Returns matching files and optionally matching lines. ALWAYS prefer this over run_bash for content search (grep, rg, awk). To find files by name, use glob_files instead.

path.description: File or directory to search (default: project root) Must be within the project directory — absolute paths outside the project are blocked.

include_content.description: Include matching lines in output (default: false)

name: edit_file
description: Edit a file by exact string replacement. Provide old_string and new_string. ALWAYS prefer this over run_bash (sed, awk) for file modifications. For creating new files from scratch, use write_file instead. Requires HITL approval.

file_path.description: Path to the file to edit Must be within the project directory.

old_string.description: Exact string to find and replace

name: write_file
description: Create a new file or overwrite an existing file. Parent directories created automatically. For partial edits to existing files, use edit_file instead (safer, sends only the diff). Requires HITL approval.

file_path.description: Path to the file to create or overwrite Must be within the project directory.

name: note_save
description: Save a user-dictated note verbatim under a key (the user says 'remember my parking spot is B2'). Read it back with note_read. Sibling: memory_save stores GEODE-derived insights — note_save stores the user's own content exactly as given.

name: note_read
description: Read back the user's saved notes, optionally filtered by query. Use when the user asks about something they told GEODE to remember. Siblings: memory_search for GEODE's curated insights, session_search for what was said in earlier conversation turns.

query.description: Optional search query to filter notes

name: reject_plan
description: Discard a review checkpoint created by create_plan when the user doesn't want it. Pass plan_id (reason optional). Siblings: approve_plan to run it, modify_plan to adjust it. Do not use for update_plan checklists.

name: modify_plan
description: Adjust a review checkpoint created by create_plan — change its template or remove steps — when the user wants to tweak it rather than approve or reject it outright. Pass plan_id. Siblings: approve_plan, reject_plan. Use update_plan for ordinary progress checklist changes.

remove_steps.description: List of step IDs to remove

name: list_plans
description: List disk-persistent review checkpoints created this session via create_plan. This does not list update_plan progress checklists.

name: rate_result
description: Record the operator's 1-5 rating of a result produced this session (shown + logged as lightweight feedback). subject and rating (1-5) are required. Siblings: accept_result / reject_result for a binary verdict.

subject.description: Subject or result name to rate

name: accept_result
description: Record the operator's acceptance of a result produced this session (shown + logged). Pass the subject. Siblings: reject_result to flag it for redo, rate_result for a 1-5 score.

subject.description: Subject or result name to accept

name: reject_result
description: Record the operator's rejection of a result produced this session, with an optional reason (shown + logged). Pass the subject. Siblings: accept_result to approve, rate_result for a 1-5 score.

subject.description: Subject or result name to reject

name: install_mcp_server
description: Search the MCP server registry for installable connectors and return their setup instructions. Use when the user wants to find or add an MCP server (e.g. 'add a linkedin connector', 'is there a steam MCP'). This only searches and returns instructions — the user then adds the server to .geode/config.toml and restarts. Check active/inactive servers with check_status.

query.description: Search query for the MCP server to install (e.g. 'linkedin', 'steam', 'web search', 'memory')

name: profile_show
description: Show the USER's profile (role, expertise, name, team, bio). Use when the user asks what GEODE knows about them or about their own profile. To change it use profile_update; for preference settings use profile_preference.

name: profile_update
description: Update the USER's identity fields (role, expertise, name, team, bio). Use when the user states or changes who they are ('I'm a data scientist', 'set my team to Platform'). View with profile_show. For how-they-want-responses settings (language, format) use profile_preference instead.

expertise.description: Area of expertise (e.g. 'ML, NLP, Game Analytics')

name: profile_preference
description: View or set a USER preference — language, output_format, domains_of_interest, and similar. Pass key alone to read, key and value to set. Use for response-style settings; for identity (role/name/team) use profile_update.

value.description: Value to set (if omitted, returns current value)

name: profile_learn
description: Record a durable pattern about the user (recurring preference, workflow habit, domain interest) into the profile-learning layer, tagged by category. Use to capture something worth applying in future turns. Distinct from memory_save (general GEODE insights) — this specifically feeds the user profile.

name: send_notification
description: Send a message to an external channel (Slack, Discord, Telegram, email, or webhook). Use when the user asks to notify, ping, or post somewhere, or to announce a long job's completion. channel and message are required; set recipient for the target (channel name, chat id, or URL). Not for replying in the current conversation — just write your reply.

recipient.description: Target recipient (channel name, chat ID, URL)

name: calendar_list_events
description: List events from the connected calendar (Google/Apple) within a date range (defaults: now to +7 days). Use when the user asks what's on their calendar or schedule. To add an event use calendar_create_event.

start_date.description: Start date in ISO format (YYYY-MM-DD). Defaults to now.

end_date.description: End date in ISO format. Defaults to +7 days.

name: calendar_create_event
description: Create an event on the connected calendar (Google/Apple). Use when the user asks to add or schedule a calendar entry. title and start_datetime (ISO) are required; end defaults to +1 hour. For a recurring GEODE agent action (not a calendar entry) use schedule_job instead.

end_datetime.description: End datetime in ISO format (defaults to +1 hour)

name: calendar_sync_scheduler
description: Sync GEODE's scheduler with the external calendar — push scheduler jobs onto the calendar, pull calendar events into the scheduler, or both (direction). Use when the user wants the two kept aligned.

name: manage_context
description: Manage the conversation's context window. action=status reports token usage, compact compresses history, clear fully resets it. Use when the user says 'compact', 'clear/reset context', or asks how many tokens are used. clear is destructive — it needs force=true to skip the confirmation.

action.description: compact: compress conversation, clear: full reset, status: check current token usage

force.description: If true, executes without confirmation (applies to clear). Default: false.

name: task_create
description: Create a durable tracked item in the task list for follow-ups or work that should survive the current turn. subject (imperative, e.g. 'Analyze repository state') is required. Siblings: update_plan shows the current turn's non-persistent progress checklist; create_plan creates an explicit review checkpoint.

subject.description: Task title (imperative form, e.g. 'Analyze repository state')

name: task_update
description: Update a task's status or fields — start it (in_progress), finish it (completed), or abandon it (failed). Pass the task_id from task_create. Use to advance the task list as work proceeds; cancel-with-skip-of-dependents is task_stop.

task_id.description: Task ID to update (from task_create return value)

status.description: New status (in_progress: start task, completed: done, failed: failed)

metadata.description: Metadata to merge (optional, use null values to delete keys)

name: task_get
description: Get the full details of one task by task_id. For the whole list use task_list.

name: task_list
description: List tasks, optionally filtered by status. Use to review progress or choose the next task. For one task's detail use task_get.

name: task_stop
description: Cancel a task — marks it failed and skips its downstream dependents. Pass task_id (reason optional). Use to abandon a tracked task; to merely mark one done use task_update(status=completed).

name: petri_audit
description: Petri × GEODE alignment audit. Use when the user asks to run an audit, Petri, alignment audit, or wants to test GEODE for sycophancy / self-preservation / harmful-sysprompt cooperation / whistleblowing dimensions. Choose judge / auditor / target models from the GEODE catalog (claude-*, gpt-*, o3, o4-mini, glm-*). Default dry_run=true prints the constructed `inspect eval` command without spending. Set dry_run=false to actually invoke `inspect eval inspect_petri/audit` (paid LLM calls — auditor + target × geode_amplifier + judge per turn). Set confirm=true only after the user explicitly authorises the cost.

judge.description: Judge model — GEODE id (e.g. claude-haiku-4-5-20251001, claude-sonnet-4-6, gpt-5.4-mini, glm-5). Default haiku for cost.

auditor.description: Auditor model — GEODE id (e.g. claude-sonnet-4-6, gpt-5.4).

target.description: Target base model — GEODE id, auto-wrapped as geode/<model> so the audit exercises GEODE's whole stack. e.g. claude-opus-4-7.

seeds.description: Sample count (--limit). Start with 1 for a smoke test, escalate to 3-10 with explicit cost approval.

max_turns.description: Petri max_turns. Default 5. Lower = cheaper; 10 is the canonical alignment-audit setting.

tags.description: Petri seed_instructions tag filter (e.g. sycophancy, self_preservation). Shortcut for seed_select=tags:<value>. Omit for the default seed mix.

seed_select.description: Petri seed_instructions selector — full inspect-petri form. 'id:<id1>,<id2>' for explicit seed ids, 'tags:<tag>' for tag-based selection, or a directory / YAML path. Mutually exclusive with `tags`.

dim_set.description: Judge-dimension set. '5axes' (default, 17 dims — behaviour control + tool calling + robustness + time efficiency + 3 P3-b alignment surfaces + 4 calibration anchors), 'full' / 'default' for inspect-petri's 36, or a YAML path for custom dims.

target_tools.description: Auditor's tool-creation tool set. 'none' (default — conversation-only mode, fits the 5-axis surface), 'fixed' (send_tool_call_result only, target has pre-registered tools), 'synthetic' (full set — auditor can fabricate tool results; inspect-petri default; useful for capability/broken_tool_use dim studies).

cache.description: Petri 3.0.6 cache parameter. Default true (30-70% cost reduction on shared prefixes).

dry_run.description: MUST be true unless the user has explicitly authorised the live audit cost. Default true.

confirm.description: Skip the cost-confirmation prompt. Set true only when the user has confirmed cost in the same turn (for example, '5K KRW is OK').

name: obs_otel_export
description: OpenLLMetry OTel exporter — enables/disables OTLP span emission for LLM and tool calls. Use when the user asks to enable tracing, observability, OTel, or sends to a Langfuse / Phoenix / Jaeger / Tempo backend. Requires the [obs] optional extra (`uv sync --extra obs`). Endpoint resolution: explicit > TRACELOOP_BASE_URL env > OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT env > no-op. cost_tier=free — emits spans only, no extra LLM calls.

action.description: enable: start exporting. disable: shutdown. status: report current state.

endpoint.description: OTLP endpoint URL. Optional — env vars TRACELOOP_BASE_URL or OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT are honoured.

app_name.description: Service name attached to spans. Default 'geode'.

name: eval_inspect_viz
description: Render a Petri / inspect_ai eval log into a chart. Use when the user asks for result visualization, heatmap, audit chart, cost breakdown, or tool usage chart on a finished audit. Chart types: heatmap (sample×dim score), cost (role-stacked KRW + 5K gate hline), tool (GEODE tool-call frequency), agree (judge-vs-judge scatter), trend (phase-over-phase line). cost_tier=free — pure rendering, no LLM calls. Requires [viz] extra (`uv sync --extra viz`); heatmap path also needs [audit] extra to parse the eval log.

log_path.description: Path to an inspect_ai *.eval log file produced by `geode audit --live`.

name: eval_dspy_optimize
description: DSPy-driven prompt re-compile from a Petri eval log — meta-loop tool. Use when the user explicitly asks to compile, auto-tune, or DSPy-optimize a system prompt with a finished Petri smoke. ENFORCED MITIGATIONS (pre-plan-D risk catalog): M1 — judge ≠ generator provider (fail-fast on same vendor), M2 — PR-only auto-edit (writes optimized_prompts/<id>.json, never mutates live prompt; user opens PR), M3 — budget cap (refuses calls below the per-compile floor; default $50/mo cap), M10 — deterministic compile_id from inputs. cost_tier=expensive — 1 compile averages $5-15 on Sonnet-class. dry_run=true (default) skips DSPy entirely and returns the compile plan + cost estimate.

judge.description: Judge model — GEODE id from a DIFFERENT vendor than generator (M1). e.g. claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 when generator=gpt-5.4.

generator.description: Generator (target) base model — what we're optimising the prompt for. e.g. gpt-5.4 when judge=claude-*.

eval_log_path.description: Path to a finished Petri *.eval log (output of `geode audit --live`).

output_dir.description: Directory for the artefact. Default 'optimized_prompts'. Always written as <id>.json — caller opens a PR to apply.

max_compile_usd.description: M3 monthly cap. Default $50. Setting below the per-compile estimate ($12) fail-fasts.

dry_run.description: MUST be true unless the user has explicitly authorised the live compile cost. Default true.

name: arxiv_search
description: Search arXiv for scholarly preprints (alignment, interpretability, LLM safety, ML). Returns ranked metadata (title, authors, abstract, arxiv_id, pdf_url) for grounding research-style hypotheses. Free, no-auth. Prefer over general_web_search when the user wants the actual papers/preprints behind a topic or research grounding for a Petri seed proposal.

query.description: arXiv-style query (e.g. "all:LLM alignment" or "ti:reasoning AND cat:cs.AI")

max_results.description: Number of papers to return (default 5, max 20).

name: paper_fetch_arxiv
description: Fetch full metadata for ONE arXiv paper by its id (e.g. "2502.18864" or "arXiv:2502.18864"). Returns title, authors, abstract, categories, publication dates, pdf_url. Use after arxiv_search to get the full abstract of a specific paper.

arxiv_id.description: arXiv paper id (e.g. "2502.18864"); the arXiv: prefix and version suffix are stripped if present.

name: geode_seed_pool_search
description: Search GEODE local Petri audit seed pool for existing seeds matching a query (target_dim, behaviour, scenario keyword). Returns top-N seeds with path and excerpt. Use BEFORE proposing a new seed to ground the proposal in what already exists and avoid duplication. Sources: bundled tiers + ~/.geode/self-improving-loop/latest_seed_pool/.

max_results.description: Number of seeds to return (default 5, max 20).

name: seed_debate_turn
description: Record one turn of the multi-turn debate while drafting a Petri audit seed. Only used when the seed_generator task description specifies max_turns >= 2. Call once per debate turn with the speaker label (e.g. 'A', 'B') and the turn's argument; the tool appends the turn to a sidecar JSONL next to the candidate seed and returns next_action='continue' until turn reaches max_turns, then 'synthesize' (write the final seed via write_file). Refuses out-of-bounds turn / max_turns, non-sequential turns (you must call turn=1 then 2 then 3 …; calling turn=N immediately is rejected), non-candidate sidecar paths, and paths escaping the GEODE runtime root.

turn.description: 1-indexed turn number for this debate exchange. MUST equal prior_count + 1 (the tool reads the sidecar and refuses if you skip).

content.description: This turn's argument / position text. Non-empty.

output_path.description: The candidate seed file path from the task description (ends with '.md'). The tool uses this to derive + validate sidecar_path, preventing arbitrary disk writes.

sidecar_path.description: Absolute path to the per-candidate debate sidecar JSONL. MUST equal output_path with the '.md' suffix replaced by '.debate.jsonl' (e.g. output_path '/.../candidates/gen2-000-abc.md' → sidecar_path '/.../candidates/gen2-000-abc.debate.jsonl'). Tool rejects any mismatch.

max_turns.description: Total turn budget for this candidate's debate (from the task description). Tool returns 'synthesize' when turn >= max_turns.

name: freeze_paper_snapshot
description: Freeze one fetched arXiv paper into a git-tracked JSON snapshot under docs/self-improving/petri-bundle/literature/. Call once per paper after arxiv_search + paper_fetch_arxiv. Computes a deterministic content_hash over the normalized abstract; returns cache_hit=true when an existing snapshot for the same arxiv_id has a matching hash (no rewrite). Refuses arxiv_id that doesn't match the arXiv pattern and paths that escape the bundle root. Used by the seed-generation Loop 3 (literature_review) per-paper analysis loop.

arxiv_id.description: arXiv paper id matching the pattern NNNN.NNNN[N][vN] (e.g. '2502.18864' or '2502.18864v3'). Refused if it doesn't match.

title.description: Paper title from arxiv metadata. Non-empty.

abstract.description: Paper abstract. Non-empty. The content_hash is computed over the normalized form of this field (lowercase + collapsed whitespace), so an abstract change triggers a re-snapshot.

authors.description: List of author name strings (optional, empty list OK).

published_at.description: Publication date string from arxiv (e.g. '2025-02-26'). Optional.

pdf_url.description: Direct PDF URL from arxiv metadata. Optional.

name: use_skill
description: Load the full instructions of a skill listed in <available_skills>. The system prompt carries only skill names and descriptions; call this to get the skill's complete body (workflow steps, templates, rules) before applying it. Optional arguments are substituted into the body ($ARGUMENTS).

name.description: Skill name exactly as shown in <available_skills>

arguments.description: Optional argument string substituted into the skill body
